fire_alarm.specialist_note: Fire alarm is typically SPECIALIST SCOPE — installed by a fire alarm contractor (e.g. ADT, Tyco, Honeywell, Kentec approved installer). M&E contractor may provide containment and builder's work only. ALWAYS confirm scope boundary.

conventional.name: Conventional (Zone) System
conventional.description: Detectors wired in zones. Panel indicates ZONE in alarm, not individual device.
conventional.typical_use: Small buildings, simple layouts. Being superseded by addressable.
conventional.cabling: 2-core FP200 per zone loop. Separate cable per zone.

addressable.name: Addressable System
addressable.description: Each detector has unique address. Panel identifies INDIVIDUAL device in alarm.
addressable.typical_use: Standard for commercial, healthcare, education. All new installations.
addressable.cabling: 2-core FP200 loop cable. Multiple devices on single loop (up to 126-250 per loop depending on manufacturer).

analogue_addressable.name: Analogue Addressable
analogue_addressable.description: Advanced addressable — each detector reports analogue signal level. Panel makes alarm decision based on trends.
analogue_addressable.typical_use: Large commercial, hospital, complex buildings. Current industry standard.

facp.name: Fire Alarm Control Panel (FACP)
facp.function: Central brain of fire alarm system. Receives signals, makes alarm decisions, activates sounders, interfaces with other systems.
specification_required: Interfaces (BMS, gas shut-off, AHU shutdown, lift recall, door release, sprinkler monitoring)
facp.count_rule: Count main panel + any repeater panels. Note number of loops/zones.

smoke_detector.name: Smoke Detector

types.optical: Optical (photoelectric) — detects visible smoke particles. Standard for most applications. Preferred for slow-burning fires.
types.ionisation: Ionisation — detects invisible combustion products. Faster response to flaming fires. RARELY used now due to radioactive source.
types.multi_sensor: Multi-sensor (optical + heat combined) — best all-round detection. Reduces false alarms. Increasingly standard.
types.beam: Beam detector — transmitter and receiver across large space. For atriums, warehouses, high ceilings (>10m).
types.aspirating: Aspirating (VESDA/HSSD) — air sampled through pipe network. Very early detection. Data centres, archives, clean rooms.

smoke_detector.count_rule: Count every detector. Note type. Category determines coverage — cross-reference BS 5839.
smoke_detector.spacing: Optical: typically 1 per 100m² (7.5m radius). Heat: 1 per 50m² (5.3m radius). Check BS 5839 tables for exact spacing by ceiling height.
smoke_detector.flag_if: Detectors shown but system category not stated — category drives detector count.

heat_detector.name: Heat Detector

types.fixed_temp: Fixed temperature — alarms at set temperature (typically 57°C or 90°C).
types.rate_of_rise: Rate of rise — alarms on rapid temperature increase. More responsive.
types.combined: Combined rate-of-rise + fixed temperature. Most common.

heat_detector.typical_use: Kitchens, plant rooms, boiler rooms, garages — where smoke detectors would cause false alarms.
heat_detector.count_rule: Count every detector. Note type. Closer spacing than smoke detectors (5.3m radius per BS 5839).

call_point.name: Manual Call Point (MCP / Break Glass)
call_point.function: Manual fire alarm activation by occupant. Red box at exit doors.
locations: At every exit door on escape routes
At every storey exit onto stairway
call_point.count_rule: Count every MCP. Note location. Check BS 5839 spacing requirements.
call_point.height: 1.4m above finished floor level (standard mounting height).

sounder.name: Sounder (Audible Alarm Device)
sounder.function: Emits audible alarm signal throughout building on fire alarm activation.

types.wall_sounder: Wall-mounted sounder. Standard for corridors, offices.
types.sounder_beacon: Combined sounder + visual beacon. Required where hearing-impaired occupants may be present (Equality Act). BS 8300.
types.beacon_only: Visual alarm device (VAD) only. For high-noise areas where sounders cannot be heard.

sounder.count_rule: Count every sounder and beacon individually. Note if combined sounder/beacon.
sounder.coverage: Sound level: minimum 65dB throughout building, 75dB in bedrooms (BS 5839). Visual beacons: visible from all positions in the room.
sounder.flag_if: No sounder/beacons shown in areas where hearing-impaired may work — flag for Equality Act compliance.

interface_unit.name: Interface Unit / Module
interface_unit.function: Connects fire alarm to other systems — input or output signal.

types.input: Input module — receives signal FROM another system (sprinkler flow switch, gas detection, smoke vent confirming open).
types.output: Output module — sends signal TO another system (gas solenoid shut-off, AHU shutdown, door hold-open release, lift recall, BMS).
types.relay: Relay module — volt-free contact for interface between systems.

interface_unit.count_rule: Count every interface. Note type (input/output) and what it connects to.
interface_unit.note: Interface units are often missed in early takeoffs. The cause-and-effect matrix defines all interfaces. If C&E matrix not provided, flag.

cable.type: FP200 Gold (standard) or MICC (enhanced fire performance)
cable.count_rule: Measure loop length from FACP around all devices and back to panel. Add 10% waste + 300mm per device for termination.

loop_configuration.addressable: Single loop cable visits all devices on the loop in sequence, returns to panel. Class A (monitored return path) or Class B (single path).
loop_configuration.conventional: Separate cable per zone. Each zone cable runs from panel to furthest device with T-offs to each detector.
loop_configuration.note: Class A (loop with return) uses approximately 2× the cable of Class B (radial). Check spec for loop class.

cable.installation: Fire-rated clips per BS 8519. Red FP200 cable. Dedicated containment or fire-rated clips. Segregated from power cables. See KB-E01 and KB-E02.

fire_alarm.categories_ref: Fire alarm system categories (L1-L5, M, P1-P2) are defined in KB-C03 under BS 5839. Category determines detector coverage extent.

access_control.specialist_note: Access control is typically specialist scope — installed by a security contractor. M&E may provide power supply, containment, and builder's work. Confirm scope boundary.

controller.name: Access Control Panel / Controller
controller.function: Controls door locks based on credential authentication. Manages access permissions, logs events.
specification_required: Number of doors controlled (2-door, 4-door, 8-door per controller)
controller.count_rule: Count every controller. Note number of doors per controller. A 4-door controller manages 4 access points.

card_reader.name: Card Reader / Credential Reader

types.proximity: Proximity card reader — HID iCLASS, MIFARE. Standard commercial.
types.smart_card: Smart card reader — contactless or contact. Higher security.
types.biometric: Fingerprint, iris, facial recognition. High security areas.
types.keypad: PIN code keypad. Simple, no card required. Often combined with card reader.
types.mobile: Bluetooth/NFC mobile credential reader. Smartphone-based access.

card_reader.count_rule: Count 1nr per access point SIDE. A controlled door with entry+exit readers = 2nr readers. Entry-only (free exit) = 1nr reader.
card_reader.note: Readers are mounted on the SECURE side of the door (where you present credential to enter).

electric_lock.name: Electric Lock

types.maglock: Electromagnetic lock (maglock) — holds door closed with magnet. Fail-safe (unlocks on power loss — fire safety). Requires: door contact, request-to-exit.
types.electric_strike: Electric strike — replaces standard strike plate. Door looks normal. Fail-secure or fail-safe available.
types.motor_lock: Motorised lock — deadbolt. Highest security. Slower operation.
types.solenoid_bolt: Solenoid bolt lock — electric bolt. Fast. Common for turnstiles, gates.

electric_lock.count_rule: Count 1nr per controlled door. Note type (maglock/strike).
electric_lock.note: Maglocks MUST be fail-safe (unlock on power loss or fire alarm activation) for fire escape doors. This is a LIFE SAFETY requirement.

door_contact.name: Door Contact / Door Position Switch
door_contact.function: Detects whether door is open or closed. Feeds back to access controller and/or fire alarm.
door_contact.count_rule: Count 1nr per controlled door. Usually concealed in door frame.

rex.name: Request to Exit (REX)
rex.function: Allows egress without card. Push button or passive infrared sensor inside controlled area.

types.button: Push-to-exit button — green button near door.
types.sensor: PIR REX — detects approach from inside. Hands-free.
types.break_glass: Emergency break-glass REX — for fire exit doors (green, not red).

rex.count_rule: Count 1nr per controlled door (on the exit side). Note type.

psu.name: Power Supply Unit (PSU)
psu.function: Provides 12V or 24V DC to locks, readers, controllers. Includes battery backup.
psu.count_rule: Count 1nr per location or per group of doors (PSU serves multiple locks). Typically 1nr per 2-4 doors.

typical_controlled_door: Share of controller (1 controller per 2-4 doors)
Share of PSU (1 PSU per 2-4 doors)
Cable: data (Cat5e/6) to reader + power cable to lock + door contact cable
per_door_summary.note: A "per door" count is useful for estimating when individual items are not shown. Multiply by number of controlled doors.

cctv.specialist_note: CCTV is typically specialist scope — installed by a security contractor. M&E may provide containment, data network switches, and power. Confirm scope boundary.

camera.name: IP Camera

types.dome: Dome camera — ceiling-mounted, vandal-resistant housing. Standard for internal. Fixed or varifocal lens.
types.bullet: Bullet camera — wall-mounted, cylindrical housing. Standard for external. Weather-resistant. Longer range.
types.ptz: PTZ camera (Pan-Tilt-Zoom) — remotely controlled movement and zoom. For large areas, car parks, perimeters. Significantly more expensive.
types.fisheye: Fisheye / panoramic — 360° view from single camera. Reduces camera count for open areas.
types.turret: Turret camera — compact dome alternative. Flexible mounting. Increasingly popular.

camera.count_rule: Count every camera. Note type and location (internal/external).

nvr.name: NVR (Network Video Recorder)
nvr.function: Records and stores video from IP cameras. Provides playback, search, export.
nvr.count_rule: Count every NVR. Note channels and storage. 1 channel per camera minimum.
nvr.note: NVR is located in comms room or security control room. Requires: data network connection, monitor, UPS power (recommended).

monitor.name: CCTV Monitor
monitor.count_rule: Count every monitor. Typically in security control room.

poe_switch.name: PoE Network Switch
poe_switch.function: Provides data connection AND power to IP cameras over Cat cable (Power over Ethernet).
poe_switch.count_rule: Count every switch. 1 PoE port per camera minimum.
poe_switch.note: May be part of main IT network or dedicated CCTV network. Check spec for network architecture.

cable.ip_system: Cat6/Cat6A — standard for IP CCTV. One cable per camera. PoE carries power and data on same cable.
cable.analogue: Coaxial (RG59/Shotgun) — legacy analogue CCTV. Separate power cable needed.
cable.measurement: m (linear). Measure from NVR/switch location to each camera position.
cable.note: IP cameras use structured cabling (Cat6) — same as data network. Count CCTV cables separately from general data cabling.

data_comms.specialist_note: Structured cabling is often by a specialist data contractor. M&E may provide containment only. However, some M&E contractors include data cabling. ALWAYS confirm scope boundary.

network_switch.name: Network Switch

types.managed_l2: Layer 2 managed — VLAN support, QoS, monitoring. Standard commercial.
types.managed_l3: Layer 3 managed — routing capability. Core/distribution switches.
types.poe: PoE switch — provides power over Ethernet to connected devices (cameras, WAPs, phones). Note PoE budget (W).

network_switch.count_rule: Count every switch. Note port count and PoE capability.

patch_panel.name: Patch Panel
patch_panel.function: Termination point for structured cabling in comms cabinet. One port per cable.
patch_panel.count_rule: Count every panel. Typically 1nr 24-port panel per 24 data outlets served.
patch_panel.note: Patch panels are mounted in server racks / comms cabinets.

comms_cabinet.name: Server Rack / Comms Cabinet

types.floor_standing: 42U full height — standard server room / main comms room.
types.wall_mounted: 6U-18U — small comms room, floor distribution. Wall-mounted.
types.open_rack: 42U open frame — data centre, where ventilation is critical.

comms_cabinet.count_rule: Count every rack/cabinet. Note size and location.

data_outlet.name: Data Outlet (RJ45)
data_outlet.function: Wall or floor outlet for data/telephone connection. Connected to patch panel via Cat cable.
data_outlet.count_rule: Count every outlet. Standard: 1× Cat6 per desk position. Many specs require 2× per position (voice + data or dual data).
data_outlet.note: Each data outlet requires: 1× Cat cable from outlet to patch panel, 1× patch panel port, 1× patch lead (not usually in M&E scope).

fibre_panel.name: Fibre Optic Patch Panel
fibre_panel.function: Terminates fibre optic cables in comms cabinet. Provides patch connections between fibre backbone and equipment.
fibre_panel.count_rule: Count every fibre panel. Typically 1nr per comms cabinet with fibre backbone connection.

wap.name: Wireless Access Point (WAP / AP)
wap.count_rule: Count every WAP. Note mounting location. Each WAP needs: 1× Cat6/Cat6A cable + 1× PoE switch port.
wap.coverage: Typical: 1 WAP per 150-250m² for standard office density. Higher density for lecture halls, conference areas.

structured_cabling.measurement: m (linear). Measure from comms cabinet to each outlet location.
structured_cabling.cable_types: Cat5e, Cat6, Cat6A — see KB-E01 for detailed specifications.
structured_cabling.count_rule: One cable per data outlet. One cable per WAP. One cable per IP camera. One cable per PoE device.
structured_cabling.waste: 15% waste + service loops at patch panel (3m loop standard).
structured_cabling.note: Cross-reference KB-E01 for cable specifications and KB-E02 for containment.

pa_va.specialist_note: PA/VA systems are typically specialist scope. Voice Alarm (VA) is a LIFE SAFETY system requiring BS 5839-8 compliance and fire-rated cable.

pa.name: Public Address (PA)
pa.function: Background music, announcements, paging. NOT life safety.
pa.standard: BS EN 60849 (being replaced by BS EN 50849)
pa.note: PA is NOT a life safety system. Standard cable and installation.

va.name: Voice Alarm (VA)
va.function: Emergency voice evacuation messages. IS a LIFE SAFETY system.
va.cable: Fire-rated cable MANDATORY (FP200 or equivalent). BS 8519 installation.
va.note: VA MUST use fire-rated cable, fire-rated containment, and meet BS 5839-8. Significantly more expensive than PA. Always confirm if system is PA or VA.
va.flag_reason: Voice Alarm is life safety. Fire-rated cable (FP200) is mandatory. Installation must comply with BS 8519. Cost is 2-3× a standard PA system.

amplifier.name: PA/VA Amplifier / Rack
amplifier.function: Central amplification unit. Processes audio, amplifies signal to speakers.
specification_required: Battery backup (mandatory for VA — 30 min minimum)
amplifier.count_rule: Count main amplifier rack + any remote amplifiers. Note total wattage and zone count.

speaker.name: Speaker / Loudspeaker

types.ceiling: Ceiling-mounted — round, flush or surface. Most common for commercial interiors.
types.wall: Wall-mounted — for corridors, staircases, external areas.
types.column: Column speaker — high output, directional. Atria, large spaces.
types.horn: Horn speaker — external, high-noise areas, car parks.
types.pendant: Pendant speaker — suspended. High ceilings, retail, atria.

speaker.count_rule: Count every speaker. Note type and power tap. VA speakers must be fire-rated.
speaker.coverage: Ceiling speakers: typically 1 per 25-40m² for speech intelligibility. Closer spacing for VA (higher coverage requirement).

microphone.name: Microphone / Paging Station

types.desk: Desk-mounted gooseneck microphone — for reception, security.
types.wall: Wall-mounted paging station — for fire control, duty manager.
types.fireman: Fireman's microphone — BS 5839-8 requirement at fire panel location.

microphone.count_rule: Count every microphone/paging point. VA systems MUST have fireman's microphone at fire alarm panel.
microphone.note: Fireman's microphone is mandatory for VA systems (BS 5839-8). Usually adjacent to the fire alarm panel.

bms.specialist_note: BMS is ALWAYS a specialist scope item. The BMS contractor designs, supplies, installs, and commissions the BMS. M&E contractor provides: power supply to BMS panels, containment for BMS cabling, mechanical equipment with BMS-compatible controls. ALWAYS confirm scope boundary — it varies significantly between projects.

controller.name: BMS Controller / DDC Panel (Direct Digital Control)
controller.function: Local intelligence — reads sensors, controls actuators, runs control sequences. Communicates with central BMS.

types.general_purpose: General purpose controller (GPC) — programmable, handles multiple plant items.
types.application_specific: Application-specific controller (ASC) — factory-programmed for specific equipment (AHU, boiler).
types.unitary: Unitary controller — small, fixed I/O count. For FCUs, VAVs, fan coils.
types.io_module: I/O expansion module — adds inputs/outputs to existing controller.

specification_required: Number of I/O points (AI, AO, DI, DO)
Communication protocol (BACnet IP, BACnet MS/TP, Modbus, LON)
controller.count_rule: Count every controller. Note I/O count and protocol. Typically 1nr per plant room or per major plant item.

field_devices.name: BMS Field Devices

sensors.temperature: Duct temp, pipe temp (strap-on/immersion/pocket), room temp, outside air temp.
sensors.pressure: Duct pressure (differential), pipe pressure, room pressure (clean rooms).
sensors.co2: CO₂ sensor — demand-controlled ventilation. Room or duct mounted.
sensors.flow: Water flow meter/switch. Air flow sensor (pitot/thermal).

actuators.valve_actuator: Motorised actuator on 2-port or 3-port control valve.
actuators.damper_actuator: Motorised actuator on duct damper (VCD, fire/smoke).
actuators.vfd: Variable frequency drive — motor speed control for pumps/fans. Sometimes M&E scope, sometimes BMS scope.

field_devices.count_rule: Count every sensor and actuator as 1nr BMS point. Note type (AI/AO/DI/DO) for each.

io_types.AI: Analogue Input — temperature, humidity, pressure, CO₂ (variable signal, typically 0-10V or 4-20mA).
io_types.AO: Analogue Output — valve position, damper position, VFD speed (variable signal).
io_types.DI: Digital Input — on/off status, alarm, switch position (binary signal).
io_types.DO: Digital Output — start/stop command, open/close (binary signal).

cabling.bacnet_ip: Cat5e/Cat6 Ethernet — standard for BACnet IP. Uses existing structured cabling network or dedicated BMS LAN.
cabling.bacnet_mstp: Screened twisted pair (STP) — RS-485 bus. BACnet MS/TP. Maximum 4000ft per segment. Up to 127 devices.
cabling.modbus: Screened twisted pair (STP) — RS-485 bus. Modbus RTU. Similar to BACnet MS/TP.
cabling.lon: LON (LonWorks) — twisted pair. Older protocol, still in use.
cabling.note: BMS network cabling is measured in m (linear). Cable type depends on protocol. Always note protocol.

head_end.name: BMS Head-End / Supervisor
head_end.function: Central PC/server running BMS software. Graphical interface, trending, alarms, scheduling.
head_end.count_rule: Count as 1nr system. Note software and licence requirements.

typically_bms_contractor: BMS field sensors (temp, humidity, CO₂, pressure)
typically_me_contractor: Motorised valves and actuators on M&E equipment
scope_boundary.rule: ALWAYS check the specification for the BMS scope boundary. It varies significantly. If unclear, flag for estimator clarification before pricing.

points_schedule.rule: If a BMS points schedule is provided, use it as AUTHORITATIVE for point counts. It lists every sensor, actuator, and status point with I/O type.
points_schedule.flag_if: No BMS points schedule provided — flag. Cannot accurately price BMS without a points schedule.
